entry.title: Privacy policy and licenses
entry.subtitle: Review information about app data, support contact, and third-party materials.

hero.title: Privacy policy and licenses
hero.subtitle: Key information about data, permissions, contact, and third-party materials.

privacy.title: Privacy policy
privacy.body: Memicard stores data needed for app functionality and personalization mainly on the user's device. This policy covers information stored locally by the app and data that the user may voluntarily provide when contacting support by email.

localData.title: Data stored locally
localData.body: The app stores locally the data required for learning progress and user features.
bullets: learning progress, statistics, and review state
pinned courses, custom courses, and custom flashcards
app settings and interface preferences
supporting data needed for features such as checkpoints or saved options
localData.note: Data is stored locally on the device mainly with SQLite and AsyncStorage.

permissions.title: Permissions and device features
permissions.body: Memicard uses device features only when needed for selected app functionality.
bullets: local notifications for study reminders are used only after the user grants permission
file access is used for data import and export
system sharing may be used when exporting data copies
the app can open the default mail client to prepare a support email
when adding custom flashcard images, the app may open the user's photo library only for that feature

contact.title: Contact
contact.body: If the user wants to report a problem or send a suggestion, the app can prepare a message in the default mail client.
bullets: the message is prepared with the support email address and a suggested subject
diagnostic details may be added to the body, such as app version, platform, OS version, and device model
the user sees the draft before sending and decides whether to send it
contact.note: Information sent by email is used only to handle the request, reply to the message, and diagnose the issue.

noTracking.title: What the app does not do
noTracking.body: Based on the current implementation, Memicard:
bullets: does not sell user data
does not use behavioral advertising
does not track activity across apps
does not send user data to its own server for normal app operation

deletion.title: Data deletion
deletion.body: The user keeps control over data stored by the app on the device.
bullets: some data can be removed inside the app, for example by deleting custom courses or resetting selected settings
uninstalling the app removes its local data from the device according to system behavior
data copies exported to files or external services remain outside the app and are managed by the user

licenses.title: Licenses and third-party materials
licenses.body: Memicard may use selected third-party materials, such as images, illustrations, or other educational assets, in accordance with their license terms or usage rules defined by the rights holders.
bullets: when attribution is required, the app provides the source and basic information about the usage conditions
some materials may be subject to additional limitations, for example regarding logos, trademarks, or presentation style
detailed terms for selected sources may be available under external links

sourceCard.title: Astronomy materials - NOIRLab
sourceCard.body: Some constellation images used in the app come from NOIRLab. Unless otherwise stated for a specific asset, these materials are used under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International license (CC BY 4.0). Source: NOIRLab. Use of these materials does not mean that NOIRLab endorses or recommends the app.

languageIntro.title: Choose your native language
languageIntro.hintDefault: Choose a language to enable the button.

step1.title: Activate course
step1.description: This is where you activate a course. Games will use flashcards from the active course.

step2.title: Course settings
step2.description: Each course has its own settings that you can change anytime.

step1.title: Hi, I'm Memicard
step1.description: I'll guide you through the app. Let's start by picking courses.

step2.title: Pin a few courses
step2.description: At start, pick courses you care about. You can pin more than one for faster access.

step3.title: After pinning, tap Next
step3.description: Bottom button will enable after you pin at least one course.

step4.title: Custom courses!
step4.description: Later you'll be able to create your own courses with your own flashcards! :3

step1.title: This is the main game
step1.description: Inspired by the Leitner system. This is where we learn words.

step2.title: Game goal
step2.description: Goal is to move a flashcard through all boxes.

step3.title: Activate a box
step3.description: By tapping a box, you activate it and draw a new flashcard.

step4.title: Give an answer
step4.description: A drawn flashcard appears. Depending on type, answer or tap a button.

step5.title: Too complicated?
step5.description: If something is unclear, check the guide on home screen via my top-bar avatar.

step2.title: Choose review mode
step2.description: Start is a quick mini-game session. Traditional is classic translation typing.

step3.title: After session
step3.description: At the end of both modes you'll see a summary table.

step1.title: Choose a course for reviews
step1.description: Tap a tile to open the game and review flashcards from this course.

step2.title: How often do they appear?
step2.description: Maybe in 2 days, maybe in a week, maybe in a year. To remember, first you need to forget ;)

step3.title: What do the numbers mean?
step3.description: Red number means overdue reviews. Green means nothing is waiting now.

step4.title: Disabling reviews
step4.description: You can disable reviews in course settings - then it won't appear on this list.

popups.noRows: No rows can be imported yet.
popups.analyzedSuccess: Great! {{validRows}} rows are ready to import.
popups.templateDirectoryMissing: No directory selected to save the CSV template.
popups.noCardsToImport: There are no cards to import now.

csvGuide.step1: 1. Prepare a CSV or TXT file with column headers.
csvGuide.step2: 2. Pick the file format below and import.
csvGuide.imagesDescription: Next to your CSV or TXT file, create an images folder. Add images there, then put file names in rows, e.g. dog.png. Supported formats: .png, .jpg, .jpeg, .svg.

selfAssess.title: 1) Self-assess
selfAssess.summary: User evaluates: I know it / I don't know it.

traditional.title: 2) Text answer
traditional.summary: Classic flashcard: prompt on front, answer on back.

frontText.description: Card front: question, keyword, or statement.

backText.note: You can enter multiple answers separated by commas or semicolons.

flip.description: If true, card will be flipped during study.

trueFalse.title: 3) True/False
trueFalse.summary: User marks whether the statement is true.

mixed.title: 4) Mixed
mixed.summary: Use multiple flashcard types in one file.

type.note: Values: traditional, true_false, self_assess (plus Polish aliases).

mixed.tip: Column layout stays the same as in sections above.

manualEditor.removeOrCloseImageForCard: Remove image or close image slot for flashcard {{index}}
